summary: Excellent Charlie Miller soundboard captures a high‑energy 1989 Irvine Meadows show with standout Blow Away and a searing second‑set jam.
review: The recording, sourced from Charlie Miller’s soundboard, is crystal clear and balances the band beautifully. The performance radiates the electric energy of that April night – Brown Eyed Women opens the set strong, Brent Mydland delivers an extended rap on Blow Away, and the second‑set jam (including a ripping He's Gone > Spoonful, The Other One and Wharf Rat) is pure Dead magic.

ai_rating.rationale: All nine reviews (aside from one zero‑star outlier) are overwhelmingly positive, praising both the superb soundboard capture and the show’s energy and highlights.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Multiple reviewers call the Charlie Miller board “better than I remember” and note great clarity, no reported drop‑outs or muddiness.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Tight, high‑energy performance with strong openers (Brown Eyed Women), playful ditties (Finiculi Finicula) and a second‑set jam that keeps the momentum soaring.

band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland’s extended rap on Blow Away and his overall keyboard/vocal presence are highlighted as major positives.
